THE GRAPEVINE RANGER STATION WAS BEING EVACUATED DUE TO FLOODING.
THE GRAPEVINE RANGER STATION WAS EVACUATED DUE TO FLOODING. EIGHT VEHICLES FULL OF VISITORS AND THREE PARK RANGERS WERE STRANDED OVERNIGHT IN THE UBEHEBE CRATER AREA.

ABOUT 20 POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN OR DAMAGED IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK DUE TO FLOODING. SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD HAS TRENCHES UP TO 6 FEET DEEP IN IT IN GRAPEVINE CANYON. AT SCOTTYS CASTLE THE POOL WAS FILLED WITH MUDDY WATER. THE HACIENDA BUILDING HERE IS FILLED WITH ONE FEET OF MUD. THE STABLES HAD MUD DEPOSITED AGAINST THE BUILDING. 3 PARK RANGERS AND 20 VISITORS WERE STRANDED OVERNIGHT AT MESQUITE SPRINGS CAMPGROUND.
DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED TWO FEET OF MUD AND DEBRIS INSIDE SCOTTY'S CASTLE VISITOR'S CENTER. TIME ESTIMATED.

MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE REPORTS SEVERAL ROAD CLOSURES IN GOLDEN VALLEY BECAUSE OF FLOODING, INCLUDING BOTH EGAR AND ESTRELLA ROADS BETWEEN SH 68 AND SHINARUMP DRIVE, TEDDY ROOSEVELT ROAD THROUGH MOST OF TOWN, SHINARUMP DRIVE FROM COLORADO ROAD TO ESTRELLA ROAD, AND CHINO DRIVE FROM ESTRELLA ROAD TO ADOBE ROAD.
MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE REPORTS OATMAN-TOPOCK HIGHWAY FROM I-40 TO MILE MARKER 40 IS CLOSED BECAUSE OF FLOODING. TIME OF CLOSURE IS AN APPROXIMATION.

EXTENSIVE FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED IN THE SCOTTY'S CASTLE AREA FROM LATE EVENING ON OCTOBER 18 TO EARLY MORNING ON OCTOBER 19. A TOTAL OF 24 POWER POLES WERE DOWNED, THE WATER TREATMENT FACILITY WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED, AND WATER LINES FROM THE SPRING UPSTREAM WERE DESTROYED. THE HISTORIC WATER STORAGE TANK WAS DAMAGED BEYOND REPAIR. THE NORTH WALL OF THE STABLES BUILDING WAS DAMAGED AND IS UNSTABLE. THE LONG HOUSE (BUNK HOUSE) WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED AND IS VERY UNSTABLE. IT IS UNKNOWN IF IT CAN BE STABILIZED. SEVERAL INCHES OF MUD, ROCK, AND DEBRIS WERE ALSO NOTED IN THE COOKHOUSE. RAINFALL ENDED AROUND 12:30 AM PDT OCTOBER 19 BASED ON RADAR.

CHP AND SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE REPORT RAPIDLY FLOWING WATER ACROSS NEEDLES HIGHWAY NEAR RIVER ROAD FORCING ROAD CLOSURES BY 145 AM PDT. INITIAL TIME ESTIMATED FROM THE COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE.
